claim: Sora 2 face cameos are 9/10 accurate when properly scanned; accurate face representation in generated video includes lighting, reflections, and tinted-surface dynamics
actionable: For video generation with character likenesses, use clean face scans (eyes, sides, straight, spoken numbers); test outputs for lighting/reflection consistency before deployment

claim: Sora 2 struggles with fine motor control: hand-object interactions morph and merge, card shuffling fails, origami folding is awkward; avoid prompts requiring dexterous manipulation
actionable: When prompting video generation, avoid detailed hand manipulation of small objects; use silhouettes, wide shots, or simpler interactions instead of close-ups of dexterous tasks

claim: Sora 2 excels at physics simulation—liquid dynamics (espresso pour, honey drip, soda bubbles), reflections in metal/glass, fire/smoke behavior are stunningly accurate
actionable: Use Sora 2 for liquid dynamics, reflective surfaces, and particle effects (smoke, snow, rain); these are production-quality and rival or exceed handcrafted VFX

claim: Sora 2 camera directions (pans, zooms, focus shifts, brightness adaptation) match prompts with high fidelity; lens behavior is realistic
actionable: In prompts, describe specific camera movements and focus/exposure transitions; Sora 2 interprets and executes these correctly, enabling cinematic control

claim: Sora 2 text/graphics rendering is inconsistent—date/number labels often wrong, bar charts misaligned, some text morphs or appears incorrectly; avoid relying on readable text/numbers
actionable: Don't use Sora 2 to generate videos with precise text, numbers, or data visualization; pre-generate these graphically or accept accuracy variance in AI output

claim: Sora 2 handles crowds and multi-person scenes with clipping and merging artifacts; background reflections remain accurate but figure consistency degrades in dense scenes
actionable: In crowd scenes, accept or hide clipping artifacts; use reflective surfaces for physics accuracy; test output with actual crowd requirements before relying on multi-person videos

claim: Sora 2 renders stylized animation (watercolor, stop-motion, Pixar-style) cleanly, avoiding morphing artifacts that plague photorealistic output—non-photo realism masks generation failures
actionable: Use stylized prompts (watercolor, anime, hand-drawn, stop-motion) to hide morphing/clipping; stylization is an effective workaround for generation weaknesses

claim: Sora 2 cameo morphing occurs in group scenes—model blends multiple faces when subjects are close or have similar features; expect variance on repeated runs
actionable: When cameo-ing multiple people, space subjects apart in frame; run multiple times and select best output; avoid pairing similar face shapes/sizes

claim: Sora 2 reflective surface rendering (prism refraction, metal sphere reflections, water reflections) is near-perfect—small details like angle-dependent light bounce are accurate
actionable: Use Sora 2 for videos requiring optical accuracy—reflections, refractions, angle-dependent light; these rival or exceed hand-crafted VFX
